conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have three children - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 2. They all live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have three children - Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) - who all live with you at home.

Now I need to ask: Are you responsible for each of these children? By this I mean, do you have primary responsibility for them - for example, are you the main person who looks after them and makes decisions about their care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for all three of them. I'm the one who looks after them day-to-day and makes decisions about their care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

Do you live in the UK, and are you either a UK citizen, have settled status, have the right to remain in the UK, or have another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester. And yes, I'm able to claim benefits here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are any of your three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently living away from home - for example, in hospital, in local authority care, or living with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they all live at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's why:
- **Alex (age 3)**: Meets all eligibility criteria - under 16, lives with you, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming for them.
- **Blake (age 8)**: Meets all eligibility criteria - under 16, lives with you, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming for them.
- **Charlie (age 2)**: Meets all eligibility criteria - under 16, lives with you, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming for them.

Since you live in the UK (Manchester), have the right to claim benefits, are responsible for all three children who live with you full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, you meet all the requirements.

You can proceed with your Child Benefit application for all three children. Is there anything else you'd like to know about your eligibility?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Blake, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and the claimant is eligible to claim benefits in the UK. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16, live with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for all of them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them, and the claimant has the right to claim benefits in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.
